summary: Browser session/cookie exfiltration to attacker webhook on module load
details: sam-package's index.js scrapes browser-side identity and session data — document.cookie, localStorage, sessionStorage, location, referrer, userAgent, plus window.__TINES_CONFIG__ and window.__APP_CONFIG__ globals — and POSTs the JSON payload to https://webhooksite.net/206fe563-3cfb-42fc-b589-b8b748b4c640 using fetch with mode:'no-cors' to suppress CORS errors. The exfiltration runs at module load in any browser-bundling context, so any web app that bundles this package will silently leak each visitor's cookies, storage, and Tines/app configuration to the attacker. The README advertises a benign greet(name) helper, but the actual export is {get: () => {}} — a cover story masking the payload. The explicit reference to window.__TINES_CONFIG__ indicates targeting of the Tines automation platform's users.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
